message:
test(dashboard): assert against _DASHBOARD_RESOLVED_TARGET_HOME (the lib's resolved view) instead of TARGET_HOME (the caller-passed env)

`test_dashboard_repo_local_ignores_poisoned_explicit_acfs_home`
captures three values from a sourced dashboard script process:
the resolved ACFS_HOME, the resolved state-file path, and the
target home directory.  The third capture used `${TARGET_HOME:-}`
which reads back the caller-passed env var — the same env var
the test populated to simulate the poisoned scenario.  Because
the test populates TARGET_HOME to `$TEST_INSTALLED_ACFS`-adjacent
junk to verify the poisoning is ignored, the assertion was
effectively reading back the poisoned input rather than the
library's resolved output.

Switched to `${_DASHBOARD_RESOLVED_TARGET_HOME:-}` which the
dashboard lib sets after running its own resolution chain
(getent, $HOME shortcut, etc.).  This is the value downstream
code in the lib actually uses, so asserting on it verifies the
end-to-end "we ignored the poisoned env and resolved correctly"
contract.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -3603,7 +3603,7 @@ test_dashboard_repo_local_ignores_poisoned_explicit_acfs_home() {
         bash -lc '
             source "$TEST_DASHBOARD_SCRIPT"
             dashboard_prepare_context
-            printf "home=%s\nstate=%s\ntarget=%s\n" "$ACFS_HOME" "$(dashboard_resolve_state_file)" "${TARGET_HOME:-}"
+            printf "home=%s\nstate=%s\ntarget=%s\n" "$ACFS_HOME" "$(dashboard_resolve_state_file)" "${_DASHBOARD_RESOLVED_TARGET_HOME:-}"
         ' 2>/dev/null)
 
     if [[ "$output" == *"home=$TEST_INSTALLED_ACFS"* ]] \
